samples.gvd:
module alu(
    input [31:0] a,
    input [31:0] b,
    input [5:0] aluc,
    output [31:0] r,
    output zero,
    output carry,
    output negative,
    output overflow,
    output flag
);

// Implement the ALU logic here

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="alu">
    <description>Implement a 32-bit ALU for a MIPS CPU.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit ALU control.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit output R.</output>
        <output id="zero">Output ZERO.</output>
        <output id="carry">Output CARRY.</output>
        <output id="negative">Output NEGATIVE.</output>
        <output id="overflow">Output OVERFLOW.</output>
        <output id="flag">Output FLAG.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="ADD" value="6'b100000">ADD</enum>
        <enum id="ADDU" value="6'b100001">ADDU</enum>
        <enum id="SUB" value="6'b100010">SUB</enum>
        <enum id="SUBU" value="6'b100011">SUBU</enum>
        <enum id="AND" value="6'b100100">AND</enum>
        <enum id="OR" value="6'b100101">OR</enum>
        <enum id="XOR" value="6'b100110">XOR</enum>
        <enum id="NOR" value="6'b100111">NOR</enum>
        <enum id="SLT" value="6'b101010">SLT</enum>
        <enum id="SLTU" value="6'b101011">SLTU</enum>
        <enum id="SLL" value="6'b000000">SLL</enum>
        <enum id="SRL" value="6'b000010">SRL</enum>
        <enum id="SRA" value="6'b000011">SRA</enum>
        <enum id="SLLV" value="6'b000100">SLLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRLV" value="6'b000110">SRLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRAV" value="6'b000111">SRAV</enum>
        <enum id="LUI" value="6'b001111">LUI</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        The module uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise. Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result. If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).
    </implementation>
endmodule
